retry_reason: The operation only executes on a non-NULL pointer; a NULL pointer is not a failure condition—there is no object to acquire a reference on, so retry is meaningless.
contract_summary: This is a get-wrapper that increments the reference count on an aa_policydb object via kref_get. If the input pointer is non-NULL, it takes a new reference and returns the same pointer; the caller must later release that reference with a corresponding put (e.g., aa_put_pdb). If the input pointer is NULL, it returns NULL without any action. The function is typically used to safely acquire an additional reference to an already-live object, ensuring the object remains valid until the caller releases it. There is no failure path—the operation always succeeds when the object exists.
